macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices jumped after Trump announced China agreed to purchase U.S. crude oil following talks with Xi.
primary_drivers: Incremental Chinese demand for US WTI/seaborne crude tightens domestic supply and lifts spot prices
Higher realized prices boost US E&P cash flow
Increased US crude export volumes benefit midstream export infrastructure and tanker shipping demand (ton-miles to Asia)

beneficiaries.rationale: Enterprise Products Partners operates the largest US crude export terminal complex on the Gulf Coast (EHT/Beaumont) plus is building the SPOT deepwater export terminal. Higher China-bound US crude flows directly drive throughput volumes and tariff revenue.

Energy Transfer operates the Nederland crude export terminal — one of the largest US Gulf Coast loading points for VLCC-sized cargoes typically destined for Asia. Higher China demand = higher dock utilization.

EOG Resources is a pure-play US onshore oil producer (Eagle Ford/Permian/Bakken) with no international exposure. Higher WTI from incremental export demand flows directly to free cash flow with minimal hedging drag.
Diamondback Energy is a pure-play Permian producer; Permian/WTI grades are the marginal barrel shipped to Asia via Corpus Christi/Houston. Direct beneficiary of widening export pull on WTI.
Frontline operates one of the largest VLCC fleets. US Gulf Coast to China is the longest-haul crude trade route (~45-day voyage), creating outsized ton-mile demand and lifting VLCC spot rates.
DHT Holdings is a pure-play VLCC operator. US-to-China crude shipments require VLCC tonnage and lengthen average voyage distance, tightening the global VLCC market.
ExxonMobil is the largest US Permian producer post-Pioneer acquisition and operates Beaumont refining/export complex. Direct upstream price uplift plus midstream throughput benefit.

losers.rationale: Delek US is a US refiner; higher WTI input costs combined with potential Asia-bound crude tightening US Gulf Coast feedstock supply compresses refining margins for inland/Gulf refiners not vertically integrated upstream.
PBF Energy is a pure-play merchant refiner with no upstream offset. Rising crude costs without immediate product price pass-through pressure crack spreads.
United Airlines has the highest jet fuel exposure as % of opex among legacy carriers and minimal hedging. Sustained crude rally lifts jet fuel costs directly.
Southwest Airlines historically maintained fuel hedges but has reduced coverage; jet fuel is ~25-30% of opex, sensitive to WTI/Brent moves.

headlines: Oil prices jump after Trump says China agreed to buy U.S. crude following Xi talks
